metadata.llm_reasoning: The symptom service frontend directly depends on shippingservice, forming a direct dependency path frontend -> shippingservice. This structure is consistent with the log agent's suspected downstream (shippingservice) and implies that a fault in shippingservice could propagate to frontend. No alternative dependency paths from frontend to other services are indicated in the topology, so the path is the sole plausible propagation route given the graph. The blast radius currently highlights frontend as affected; frontend is high criticality, making monitoring of the symptom service essential, while shippingservice remains upstream and not within the immediate blast radius.

result.synthesis_reasoning: All sources show minimal to no concrete fault signals. The Log Agent reports no errors in the window and cannot identify a root-cause path; Topology shows a plausible but not causally confirmed path from shippingservice to frontend; TCB-RCA places frontend as the initial node with no detected anomalies. With no observed upstream error indicators or logs, and given the absence of a contradictory strong signal from the logs, we treat frontend as the most plausible root-cause candidate only as a conservative, low-confidence conclusion. If additional time-window logs appear or upstream service logs become available (e.g., shippingservice or productcatalogservice reporting faults), reevaluate to possibly elevate upstream root-cause candidates or identify an unobserved root cause per the guidelines.
